app_map.capture_sources:
Two capture pipelines, both producing the identical on-disk `capture/` contract (rgb/*.png lossless, depth/*.npy float32 [H,W] meters NaN=invalid, confidence/*.png mask, intrinsics/timestamps/metadata JSON; poses.json ONLY when a pose stream exists). All paths absolute under .../stages/stage1_capture/AnatomyCapture/.

PATH A — ARKit (default). File: SessionCoordinator.swift (the ARSessionDelegate) driven by CaptureModel.swift (owns the ARSession, `makeConfiguration()` lines 129-148). Records per kept frame (SessionCoordinator.session(_:didUpdate:) lines 82-145): RGB from `frame.capturedImage` (colorW/H read at lines 110-111), LiDAR depth from `frame.smoothedSceneDepth ?? frame.sceneDepth` (line 107), a confidence mask thresholded at ARConfidenceLevel>=medium (confidenceThreshold=1, set in CaptureModel init line 103), per-frame intrinsics K from `frame.camera.intrinsics` (line 120), and a metric camera pose R/t from `frame.camera.transform` via Conventions.openCVCameraToWorld (line 126). So ARKit = RGB + smoothed LiDAR depth + confidence + pose. Also runs sceneReconstruction=.mesh for the live coverage overlay (feedback only, not saved).

PATH B — AVFoundation 'HQ-Depth'. File: AVFoundationCaptureSource.swift (AVCaptureDataOutputSynchronizerDelegate), owns its OWN AVCaptureSession on `.builtInLiDARDepthCamera`. Records (dataOutputSynchronizer lines 257-328): RGB from the synchronized video sample buffer, RAW absolute LiDAR depth from AVCaptureDepthDataOutput (DepthFloat16 converted to Float32, `isFilteringEnabled=false` so holes are NaN, line 111), K from `depthData.cameraCalibrationData.intrinsicMatrix` scaled to color res (lines 293-303). It writes R:nil, t:nil (line 312) — NO pose; the Linux pipeline recovers pose via unseeded SfM. Confidence: no per-pixel confidence; mask is finite>0. Both feed the same FrameWriter and the same KeyframeSelector.
app_map.mode_selection: A single runtime enum switch, `CaptureBackend` (CaptureMetadata.swift lines 4-12: `.arkit` | `.hqDepth`). State lives in CaptureModel.backend (CaptureModel.swift line 40, default .arkit). The user picks it via a segmented Picker in CaptureView.setupControls (CaptureView.swift lines 134-138), bound get=model.backend / set=model.setBackend($0). CaptureModel.setBackend(_:) (lines 170-181) is the switch logic: only allowed in idle/previewing (guard line 171); it tears down the OUTGOING backend first (ARKit `session?.pause()` / HQ `avSource.stopPreviewAndWait()`, lines 174-177) so only one owns the rear camera, flips `backend`, and resets focus. The SwiftUI preview branches on backend in CaptureView.body (lines 35-39): ARKit -> ARPointCloudView, else -> AVCapturePreview(source: model.avSource). Derived facts hang off the enum: backend.providesPose and backend.uiLabel (CaptureMetadata.swift 8-11). There is NO resolution or LiDAR-on/off selection today — LiDAR is implicit in both backends and always on; ARKit uses the default video format, HQ auto-picks the max-area depth-capable format.
app_map.lidar_depth_capture:
ARKit LiDAR: enabled in CaptureModel.makeConfiguration() (CaptureModel.swift lines 133-139) via `cfg.frameSemantics = [.smoothedSceneDepth]` (fallback `.sceneDepth`), captured per frame in SessionCoordinator line 107 (`frame.smoothedSceneDepth ?? frame.sceneDepth`), turned into (depth, mask) by PixelBufferCopy.depthAndMask with confidence thresholding (line 113-114). Depth is temporally smoothed + confidence-gated.

HQ LiDAR: AVFoundationCaptureSource.configureIfNeeded() (lines 79-154) sets `session.sessionPreset = .inputPriority` (line 96, required so activeFormat isn't overridden), adds AVCaptureDepthDataOutput with `isFilteringEnabled=false` (line 111), picks the highest-area color format whose `supportedDepthDataFormats` is non-empty and not binned (lines 116-123), then its highest-area DepthFloat16 depth format (lines 124-132), and sets device.activeFormat/activeDepthDataFormat (lines 135-136). Per frame it converts to Float32 and finite-masks (line 288). Raw, absolute, unfiltered.

Export (both paths, FrameWriter.swift writeFrame lines 55-99): depth -> depth/%06d.npy via NumpyWriter.npyData as float32 [H,W], NaN=invalid, meters; confidence/mask -> confidence/%06d.png (PngWriter.pngGray, 255/0); RGB -> rgb/%06d.png lossless. Pose is optional: `if let R=p.R, let t=p.t` (line 87) accumulates posesJSON; poses.json is written ONLY if non-empty (lines 117-124), so HQ omits it and metadata `has_poses=false`. Per-frame K persisted for all frames (focus-breathing tracking).
app_map.video_resolution:
There is NO explicit resolution/videoFormat selection anywhere (grep for videoFormat/supportedVideoFormats/recommendedVideoFormat/captureHighResolution across the app returns zero hits). Actual color resolution is whatever each source's buffer reports; FrameWriter records it from p.colorW x p.colorH (FrameWriter.swift line 95, color_resolution) and prints it in the README (line 145).

ARKit path: uses the DEFAULT ARWorldTrackingConfiguration.videoFormat (makeConfiguration never sets `cfg.videoFormat`). On LiDAR iPhone Pro devices the depth-enabled default is typically ~1920x1440 (4:3, '1080p-class'), read via CVPixelBufferGetWidth/Height on frame.capturedImage (SessionCoordinator.swift 110-111). No 4K path exists.

HQ path: NOT fixed — configureIfNeeded picks the MAX-AREA color format that also carries depth and isn't binned (AVFoundationCaptureSource.swift 116-123), so it's the highest streaming resolution the LiDAR depth camera supports simultaneously with depth (device-dependent, commonly up to ~1920x1440). It is a synchronized video stream, NOT a full-res photo/still — despite the 'HQ' name there is no AVCapturePhotoOutput. So 'HQ-stills' as requested does not exist yet.
app_map.frame_cap:
Authoritative cap lives in KeyframeSelector.swift (a struct, lines 11-16): `maxKeyframes: Int = 60`, `budgetSeconds: TimeInterval = 20`, `timeStride: TimeInterval = 20.0/48` (~0.417 s, targets ~48 frames), `angleGateRadians = 7.5deg`. Selection logic: shouldKeep(rotation:time:) (lines 42-64) keeps a frame when rotation exceeds the 7.5deg angular gate OR the time-stride elapses (HQ has no live pose so identity rotation -> time-stride only, AVFoundationCaptureSource.swift line 275). isFinished(now:) (lines 36-38) returns true when `kept >= maxKeyframes` OR `elapsed >= budgetSeconds`; both delegates check it and call model.finishFromBudget() (SessionCoordinator.swift 94-98, AVFoundationCaptureSource.swift 268-272). Each backend owns its own KeyframeSelector instance (SessionCoordinator.swift line 30, AVFoundationCaptureSource.swift line 54), both created with the no-arg `KeyframeSelector()` and reset in startWriting.

SECOND, redundant cap: CaptureModel.budgetSeconds (CaptureModel.swift line 82 = 20) drives (a) the safetyStop backstop Task that force-stops at budgetSeconds+1 (lines 203-206) and (b) the UI progress bar + 'x / 20 s' label (CaptureView.swift lines 122-123, 165). These are SEPARATE constants from KeyframeSelector's — raising the cap requires changing BOTH or capture stops at whichever is smaller. timeStride=20/48 is also hardcoded to the 20 s/48-frame assumption, so raising budgetSeconds without raising maxKeyframes will just hit the 60 cap.
app_map.transmission:
Off-device path (nothing auto-sends; review screen only). Zip: Exporter.zip(directory:) (Exporter.swift 9-27) uses NSFileCoordinator .forUploading to produce a unique temp .zip. Upload: Uploader.swift — Uploader.shared.upload(zipURL:sessionID:pin:) (lines 39-59) does a BACKGROUND URLSession uploadTask POST to `<baseURL>/upload?session=<id>`, header `Authorization: Bearer <token>`, `Content-Type: application/zip`, and per-transmit `X-Upload-Pin: <pin>` (line 52). Config in UploadConfig (lines 10-19): baked Info.plist UPLOAD_BASE_URL/UPLOAD_TOKEN win, else UserDefaults keys upload_base_url/upload_token from SettingsView; isConfigured requires both. Completion (didCompleteWithError 62-76) treats HTTP 401 as rejected (wrong PIN/token), forwarded via onComplete to CaptureModel.uploadMessage (CaptureModel init 105-107).

Orchestration in CaptureModel.swift: transmit() (315-322) guards UploadConfig.isConfigured + lastCaptureDir, then shows the PIN prompt (showPINPrompt=true) — nothing sent yet. The prompt is an .alert in CaptureView.swift (lines 52-60; hint 'Paul's Locker Combination', server-side UPLOAD_PIN=6250). Its Send button calls confirmTransmit(pin:) (326-345): syncDescriptionToDisk() rewrites metadata.json, waits for any in-flight finalize zip, rebuilds a fresh zip, then startUpload(_:pin:) (347-352). Alternate manual path: ShareLink on exportURL (AirDrop / Save to Files) + the session folder is always visible in Files (Info.plist). Zip building is serialized via zipTask + isPreparingUpload re-entrancy guard.
app_map.settings_ui:
Two distinct surfaces. (1) SettingsView.swift (gear sheet, opened from CaptureView.topBar line 76) is MINIMAL — only two @AppStorage fields, upload_base_url + upload_token (lines 8-9); no capture-mode settings at all. (2) The real capture toggles live inline in CaptureView.swift, driven by @Observable CaptureModel state, and are pre-record only (locked during recording via the phase switch):
 - Backend segmented Picker in `setupControls` (CaptureView.swift 132-150), bound to model.backend via model.setBackend.
 - `showOverlay` toggle (CaptureModel line 52) — topBar button lines 96-105, ARKit only, display-only.
 - `focusLocked` toggle (CaptureModel lines 57-59, didSet forwards to avSource.setFocusLocked) — topBar button lines 108-118, HQ only.
 - orientation is AUTO-detected at record time (currentInterfaceOrientation, CaptureModel 397-400), no picker.
 - captureDescription TextField (setupControls 143-145 and review screen 228-232).
CaptureModel is the single source of truth for all bindable toggles (backend/orientation/showOverlay/focusLocked/captureDescription, lines 39-59). New toggles should follow this pattern: add @Observable state on CaptureModel, expose a mutator that guards phase==idle/previewing, and surface either as a Picker/Toggle in setupControls (pre-record) or a Section in SettingsView.
app_map.edit_plan:
Concrete edits (files + symbols). All under .../stages/stage1_capture/AnatomyCapture/.

(a) CAPTURE-MODE {ARKit-1080p, ARKit-4K, HQ-stills} x {LiDAR on/off}
1. CaptureMetadata.swift: add `enum CaptureMode: String, CaseIterable, Sendable { case arkit1080, arkit4K, hqStills }` with `uiLabel` and computed `backend: CaptureBackend` (arkit1080/arkit4K->.arkit, hqStills->.hqDepth). In CaptureMetadata add `var captureMode: CaptureMode` and `var lidarEnabled: Bool`; extend dictionary() (lines 43-58) with "capture_mode" and "lidar_enabled"; when lidarEnabled==false set providesPose/depth_source accordingly.

CaptureModel.swift: add `var captureMode: CaptureMode = .arkit1080` and `var lidarEnabled: Bool = true` (near lines 40-59); make `backend` a derived `{ captureMode.backend }` (keeps CaptureView topBar branches working). Replace setBackend(_:) (170-181) with `setMode(_:)` that tears down/rebuilds only when the derived backend changes, and calls runConfiguration() (150-152) when only resolution/LiDAR changed. In makeConfiguration() (129-148): pick `cfg.videoFormat` — for arkit1080 choose a ~1920x1080/1440 entry from `ARWorldTrackingConfiguration.supportedVideoFormats`; for arkit4K use `ARWorldTrackingConfiguration.recommendedVideoFormatForHighResolutionFrameCapturing` OR a 4K supportedVideoFormats entry. AVFoundationCaptureSource.swift: implement HQ-stills as a NEW `AVCapturePhotoOutput` with `isDepthDataDeliveryEnabled=true` (full-res still + depth), added in configureIfNeeded() (79-154) alongside/instead of the streaming videoOut/depthOut, triggered on the keyframe gate; reuse the existing K-scaling (293-303) and FramePayload append (309-313). (b) RAISE maxKeyframes / budgetSeconds — TWO sources must move together
5. KeyframeSelector.swift (lines 13-16): raise `maxKeyframes` and `budgetSeconds`, and recompute `timeStride = budgetSeconds / targetFrames` (don't leave 20.0/48). Best: add `init(maxKeyframes:budgetSeconds:)` and thread values from CaptureModel so there is ONE source of truth; update the two instantiations (SessionCoordinator.swift line 30, AVFoundationCaptureSource.swift line 54) or set them in each startWriting.
6. CaptureModel.swift line 82 `let budgetSeconds` -> raise to match (or derive both from one constant); this auto-updates the safetyStop backstop (203-206) and the UI progress/label in CaptureView.swift (122-123, 165) which already read model.budgetSeconds. Verify KeyframeSelector.budgetSeconds and CaptureModel.budgetSeconds stay equal, else capture stops at the smaller one.

No Info.plist changes needed (camera usage keys already present). Metadata/README color_resolution (FrameWriter.swift 95,145) auto-reflects whatever buffer size the new videoFormat/photo path yields, so no writer change required.

arkit.can_4k_with_lidar:
PARTIAL → effectively YES; the teammate's "4K video OR LiDAR, pick one" premise is NOT supported by any Apple documentation. Two separate truths:

(1) HIGH-RES-STILL PATH — DEFINITIVELY YES, Apple-confirmed. You run a normal LiDAR session with .sceneDepth/.smoothedSceneDepth AND set videoFormat = recommendedVideoFormatForHighResolutionFrameCapturing (a 1920x1440@60fps stream), then pull 12MP (4032x3024) stills on demand with captureHighResolutionFrame(). An Apple Frameworks Engineer states depth is available on those high-res frames via frame.sceneDepth.depthMap (Apple Developer Forums thread 805839). Depth + high-res color coexist.

(2) CONTINUOUS 4K-STREAM PATH (3840x2160@30fps via recommendedVideoFormatFor4KResolution) + per-frame sceneDepth — Apple neither documents support NOR any exclusion. Architecturally there is no reason it cannot: 4K RGB comes from the wide camera while depth comes from the LiDAR sensor (fused), so they are independent capture paths; sceneDepth's depthMap stays 256x192 regardless of RGB resolution; and supportsFrameSemantics(.sceneDepth) is a DEVICE-level check, not gated per video format. WHY UNCERTAIN: no Apple doc and no first-party sample explicitly demonstrate the 4K continuous format streaming sceneDepth every frame, and recommendedVideoFormatForHighResolutionFrameCapturing (not the 4K one) is the format Apple pairs with depth in its guidance. So the only load-bearing residual risk is that the 4K format is one you must set, and only ONE videoFormat can be active — 4K-stream and the high-res-capture stream are mutually exclusive AS FORMATS (this, plus AR Foundation abstractions, is the most likely origin of the teammate's "either/or" belief). Bottom line for the teammate: you do NOT have to sacrifice LiDAR to get high-resolution color. The safe, Apple-blessed way to get BOTH continuous LiDAR depth AND high-resolution color is the high-res-frame-capture path (1440p@60 stream + sceneDepth + periodic 12MP stills). If you specifically want a continuous 4K stream WITH per-frame depth, treat it as unverified-by-docs and confirm on-device (see resolution_options).
arkit.exact_apis:
- ARWorldTrackingConfiguration (subclass of ARConfiguration) — the config that supports both LiDAR depth and these video formats.
- class var ARConfiguration.recommendedVideoFormatFor4KResolution: ARConfiguration.VideoFormat? — returns the 4K (3840x2160@30fps) format, or nil if device/config unsupported. iOS 16+.
- class var ARWorldTrackingConfiguration.recommendedVideoFormatForHighResolutionFrameCapturing: ARConfiguration.VideoFormat? — returns the stream format best suited to on-demand high-res still capture (1920x1440@60fps stream, 12MP stills). iOS 16+. (Obj-C doc id 3930047.)
- var ARConfiguration.videoFormat: ARConfiguration.VideoFormat — you assign one of the above (only ONE at a time).
- class var ARConfiguration.supportedVideoFormats: [ARConfiguration.VideoFormat] — enumerate to inspect imageResolution / framesPerSecond / captureDeviceType.
- ARConfiguration.VideoFormat (a.k.a. ARVideoFormat) with property var isRecommendedForHighResolutionFrameCapturing: Bool.
- struct ARConfiguration.FrameSemantics with options .sceneDepth, .smoothedSceneDepth, .personSegmentationWithDepth.
- class func ARConfiguration.supportsFrameSemantics(_:) -> Bool — DEVICE-level capability check (true on LiDAR devices); and var frameSemantics: ARConfiguration.FrameSemantics to enable them.
- var ARFrame.sceneDepth: ARDepthData? and var ARFrame.smoothedSceneDepth: ARDepthData? → ARDepthData exposes .depthMap (CVPixelBuffer, 256x192, meters) and .confidenceMap.
- func ARSession.captureHighResolutionFrame(completion:) — iOS 16+; captures a single high-res photo while the video stream keeps running uninterrupted.
- func ARSession.captureHighResolutionFrame(using: AVCapturePhotoSettings, completion:) — iOS 16+; NOTE per Apple engineer: custom AVCapturePhotoSettings are only partially honored — e.g. photoSettings.isDepthDataDeliveryEnabled is NOT supported here and throws; use default settings and read depth from frame.sceneDepth.depthMap instead.
- var ARFrame.capturedImage: CVPixelBuffer (the RGB); var ARFrame.capturedDepthData: AVDepthData? is non-nil ONLY for ARFaceTrackingConfiguration (TrueDepth front cam), NOT for world tracking — for world/LiDAR depth always use frame.sceneDepth.
arkit.device_os_requirements:
- LiDAR / sceneDepth: iOS 14+ on LiDAR-equipped devices only = iPhone 12 Pro / Pro Max and every later Pro / Pro Max, plus iPad Pro (2020, LiDAR) and later. Non-Pro iPhones have NO LiDAR, so sceneDepth is unavailable there regardless of resolution.
- 4K video format (recommendedVideoFormatFor4KResolution): iOS 16+, on iPhone 11 and later and any iPad Pro with the M1 chip (per WWDC22 'Discover ARKit 6'). Returns nil otherwise.
- captureHighResolutionFrame + recommendedVideoFormatForHighResolutionFrameCapturing: iOS 16+ (ARKit 6).
- NET for doing 4K color AND LiDAR on the SAME device: you need an iPhone Pro (12 Pro or newer) running iOS 16+ — Pro models satisfy both the LiDAR requirement and the iOS-16/A-series requirement for the 4K format. A base (non-Pro) iPhone can do 4K but has no LiDAR at all.
arkit.resolution_options:
RGB video-format options on a LiDAR iPhone Pro (enumerate ARConfiguration.supportedVideoFormats; example values reported for iPhone 13 Pro Max):
- 1920x1440 @ 60fps (typical default / high-res-capture stream format; captureDeviceType = builtInWideAngle).
- 1920x1080 @ 60fps (16:9 HD).
- 3840x2160 @ 30fps — the 4K format (pixelFormat 420f, builtInWideAngle). 4K halves frame rate to 30fps vs 60fps for the HD/1440p formats.
- 12MP still = 4032x3024 delivered on demand via captureHighResolutionFrame (not a continuous stream).

DEPTH is decoupled from RGB resolution: ARKit sceneDepth.depthMap is always 256x192, delivered per ARFrame (up to 60Hz on the 60fps formats; it will arrive at the stream's frame cadence, so ~30Hz if the 4K@30 format is active). It does NOT scale up with 4K color. (For reference, the separate AVFoundation LiDAR path, AVCaptureDeviceTypeBuiltInLiDARDepthCamera, yields 320x240@30fps — higher-res depth but outside ARKit's fused world-tracking pipeline.)

RUNTIME VERIFICATION for the continuous-4K + per-frame-depth question (since docs are silent): on-device, set config.videoFormat = recommendedVideoFormatFor4KResolution, add .sceneDepth to frameSemantics, call session.run, then confirm session.currentFrame?.sceneDepth != nil across several frames. If ARKit rejects the combination it will surface a session error / omit sceneDepth — this is the one-line empirical test that settles it for your target device+OS.
arkit.tradeoffs:
- Frame rate: the 4K format streams at 30fps (vs 60fps for the 1440p/1080p formats). If you use the 4K continuous format, sceneDepth (and everything else) is delivered at that 30Hz cadence, so you trade half your depth/pose update rate for 4K color. The high-res-capture format keeps the stream at 60fps and only the on-demand 12MP still costs a brief capture.
- Depth resolution/quality does NOT change with 4K: sceneDepth stays 256x192; you are not getting '4K depth.' 4K only sharpens the RGB.
- captureHighResolutionFrame: momentary 12MP grab; Apple states the video stream runs uninterrupted, and depth remains available via frame.sceneDepth.depthMap. KNOWN BUG: on some devices (reported iPad Pro 6th-gen / 2022) the 256x192 sceneDepth map does not register/align correctly to the 12MP capturedImage, yielding over-estimated distances — validate alignment before trusting metric depth on high-res stills.
- Thermal/memory/bandwidth: 4K frames are ~8.3MP each; sustained 4K + per-frame LiDAR fusion + your reconstruction load raises memory bandwidth and heat, and iOS will thermally throttle frame rate under prolonged capture. For a clinical capture session, budget for thermal drop and larger per-frame buffers.
- Format exclusivity: only one videoFormat is active at a time; the 4K-stream format and the high-res-capture-stream format are mutually exclusive choices — this format-level either/or is the real constraint (not a color-vs-LiDAR one).
arkit.high_res_photo_capture: captureHighResolutionFrame(completion:) (iOS 16+) is the intended way to get periodic full-resolution stills mid-session. WWDC22 'Discover ARKit 6' states ARKit captures the single photo 'on demand in the background, while the video stream is running uninterrupted' — so it does NOT tear down or pause the AR session, and it does NOT stop LiDAR: tracking and sceneDepth keep running. The returned ARFrame's capturedImage is the 12MP (4032x3024) still, and because you enabled .sceneDepth, that frame also carries LiDAR depth via frame.sceneDepth.depthMap (256x192). Apple guidance (forums thread 805839): do NOT try to force depth through custom AVCapturePhotoSettings.isDepthDataDeliveryEnabled (unsupported, throws) and do NOT expect frame.capturedDepthData (that field is TrueDepth/face-tracking only) — read frame.sceneDepth.depthMap with default photo settings. For best still quality, set videoFormat = recommendedVideoFormatForHighResolutionFrameCapturing first. Caveat: verify sceneDepth-to-12MP-image alignment (known registration bug on some iPad Pro units). This path is the strongest recommendation for a workflow that wants both continuous LiDAR depth AND periodic high-resolution color without an either/or sacrifice.

metric.sfm_scale_ambiguity: Monocular structure-from-motion (and monocular SLAM/GS) reconstructs a scene only up to a SIMILARITY transform — 7 gauge degrees of freedom: 3 translation + 3 rotation + 1 global scale — all unobservable from images alone. A 3D point cloud and a uniformly scaled copy project to identical pixel coordinates, so reprojection error is completely blind to global scale (Strasdat et al., RSS 2010, "Scale Drift-Aware Large Scale Monocular SLAM"; the monocular-VIO scale paper arXiv:2603.26740 states the accelerometer "breaks the similarity invariance of vision alone"). This is exactly consistent with your own pipeline finding (finding-pose-not-ceiling-scale-ambiguity): three independent pose solutions agree to ~1-2mm and 1.8px reprojection pins per-frame RELATIVE pose to sub-mm, yet reprojection "is blind ONLY to a single global scale." So the shape/relative geometry your SfM+GS pipeline produces is already sub-mm; the ONLY thing images cannot fix is the one scalar that turns that shape into millimeters. Absolute scale therefore requires an EXTERNAL metric reference: a known object dimension (ruler/fiducial), a calibrated stereo baseline (what Vectra has built in), or a metric motion/depth sensor (LiDAR, IMU-derived camera baseline). Reference: Strasdat RSS 2010; arXiv:1611.09498 (inertial scale for mobile SfM); MDPI Appl. Sci. 15(10):5361 (monocular metric scaling).
metric.markerless_options: Three device-sensor routes set absolute scale WITHOUT any physical marker, and all are already present in an ARKit capture: (1) iPhone LiDAR (dToF) — returns a per-pixel metric depth map (256x192, ML-fused with RGB). Scale is set by anchoring the unit-less SfM depths to LiDAR depth at the same pixels; a single well-measured LiDAR range on the object directly fixes the 1-DoF scale. Direct, dense, no motion required, but coarse-resolution and biased in the near field (see below). (2) ARKit VIO camera-path baseline — the accelerometer measures gravity-referenced metric acceleration; because gravity has fixed magnitude while body acceleration scales with the unknown factor, double-integrating the inertial signal yields a METRIC inter-frame translation (the poses ARKit reports are already in meters). SfM scale is then locked so the reconstructed camera baseline equals the ARKit metric baseline. This is scale from the camera-motion path, not from the object, so it is immune to LiDAR's near-field depth bias — which is why your pipeline's decision to trust camera_path (VIO=1.000) over LiDAR (1.127) at close range was correct. (3) Known IMU motion / trajectory baseline — the general form of (2): any metric-length camera translation recovered from IMU (or a deliberate known displacement) fixes scale. Critical caveat from arXiv:2603.26740: VIO scale is only observable when translational acceleration is non-zero — a phone hovering near a static object with tiny straight moves gives near-zero excitation and poorly conditioned scale.
metric.accuracy_by_method: LiDAR anchor: absolute accuracy ~±1cm for objects >10cm side length, precision ~1cm; detection degrades below ~10cm and fails near ~5cm (Scientific Reports / PMC8593014, iPhone 12 Pro). Close-range RMSE ~4.89mm vs 3.44mm terrestrial-laser reference (iPhone 13 Pro, UNB TCRC), ~5mm mean deviation close range (Vogt 2021). So the LiDAR floor is a few-mm to ~1cm absolute, NOT sub-mm; the 256x192 ML-completed depth adds edge/long-tail noise (ARKitScenes). VIO baseline: excellent relative trajectory (RPE ~0.02 m/s, final-drift 0.14-0.79m over 84-145m indoor; best of ARKit/ARCore/T265/ZED2 — PMC9785098/arXiv:2207.06780), but the relevant number is SCALE error: monocular-VIO literature reports ~4.8-9.2% depending on motion (straight-line 9.2%, circular 6.4%, figure-eight 4.8% — arXiv:2603.26740). With good excitation ARKit scale is realistically a few percent (~2-5%); for small near-object motions it is worse. Ruler / known fiducial (validation only): a direct metric constraint at the object gives <1% / sub-mm — this is why it is the gold-standard tiebreaker. Clinical anchor points vs Vectra: phone TrueDepth/LiDAR reaches ~0.86-1.1mm RMS landmark deviation vs 3dMD/Vectra, but Bland-Altman 95% limits of agreement frequently exceed ±3mm (PMC11723895 Vectra M5; PMC10882018; jprasurg Vectra H2) — i.e. the phone lands in the 1-3mm band, borderline for the clinical ±2mm criterion.
metric.lidar_nearfield_bias: Yes — the ~10-12% LiDAR-vs-VIO near-field discrepancy is real, known, and a CAPTURE-DISTANCE artifact, not a pipeline defect. Your own data nails it: LiDAR anchor scale 1.127 vs VIO 1.000 = 11.95% at 203mm working distance (sunglasses), shrinking to 1.035 vs 1.000 = 3.47% at 323mm (hand), with per-frame residual growing as the camera gets closer (corr -0.41). This matches the literature: ARKit's rear LiDAR has a reliable MINIMUM range of only ~0.25-0.26m (front TrueDepth ~0.22m; Apple ARDepthData docs; it-jim analysis), and the 12% error appears precisely because the 203mm capture sat BELOW that minimum. Mechanism: at close range the coarse 256x192 dToF returns are ML-fused/interpolated with RGB, and the completion/edge handling biases depth (long-tail noise on projection edges); performance also degrades for small objects (<10cm) — both regimes your near-field captures hit. Mitigations, in priority order: (1) capture at >=250mm so LiDAR enters its reliable range (your data shows anchors then nearly agree); (2) when below ~250mm, prefer the VIO camera-path scale, which does not depend on object depth and is gravity-anchored; (3) robust scale lock — take the MEDIAN LiDAR/SfM ratio over only high-confidence depth pixels (ARKit confidenceMap), discarding edges and low-confidence returns, rather than a mean; (4) temporal averaging of the scale ratio across many frames to beat down per-frame LiDAR noise (~1cm); (5) periodic ruler/known-size calibration to detect residual bias.
metric.is_markerless_mm_possible: Definitively: markerless SHAPE at sub-mm is achievable (your SfM/GS already reconstructs relative geometry to ~1-2mm, and reprojection pins it there), but markerless sub-mm/sub-1% ABSOLUTE SCALE from phone sensors alone is NOT reliably achievable. The honest realistic floor for absolute scale, markerless, is roughly 2-5%: about 2-5% via ARKit VIO with deliberately excited motion (figure-eight, varied curvature — floor ~4.8%, arXiv:2603.26740), or ~1-2% / ~1cm via LiDAR robustly locked at >=250mm (±1cm absolute for >10cm objects, PMC8593014). Best case, fusing a >=250mm LiDAR median anchor with a well-excited VIO baseline and temporal averaging, you can push toward the ~2-4% band — which is a few mm on a 10cm feature, i.e. right at the edge of the clinical ±2mm bar and NOT a guaranteed 1mm. The single scalar that images cannot see (7th gauge DoF) is the hard limiter, and both markerless sensors that can supply it top out at the low-percent level, not sub-percent. To guarantee sub-1%/sub-mm absolute scale you still need a physical metric reference (ruler/known-size fiducial) or many-capture averaging. Bottom line: markerless gets you competitive few-percent absolute scale and sub-mm shape, but a hard 1mm-absolute guarantee is not markerless-achievable with today's iPhone sensors.
metric.recommendation: Adopt a markerless "VIO-primary, LiDAR-secondary, ruler-as-QA" scale strategy. (1) PRIMARY scale = ARKit VIO camera-path baseline: it is gravity-anchored, metric, and immune to LiDAR near-field bias — your camera_path=1.000 choice was correct. To make it well-conditioned, prescribe capture MOTION with real acceleration excitation (a figure-eight / varied-curvature orbit, not a static hover or straight slide); this alone roughly halves VIO scale error (9.2%->4.8%). (2) SECONDARY/cross-check scale = LiDAR, used ONLY at >=250mm working distance, via a robust MEDIAN of the LiDAR/SfM depth ratio over high-confidence pixels, with temporal averaging across frames; enforce your existing anchors_disagree flag and, when they disagree at close range, defer to VIO. (3) Ruler / known-size fiducial = VALIDATION-ONLY: keep it out of the clinical scene, but run it periodically as ground-truth QA to measure residual absolute-scale error and to detect drift — never as a required in-frame marker. Expectation-setting vs Vectra/Adrian: Vectra's edge is a permanently calibrated stereo baseline (built-in metric scale, sub-mm), effectively a hardwired "marker." Your differentiator is portability + markerless + full-surface GS, at a realistic cost of few-percent absolute scale. Market/validate against the ~1-3mm clinical band the smartphone-vs-Vectra studies report, not a 1mm absolute claim. If a specific metric truly needs 1mm absolute, the only honest path is a one-time known-size calibration per session or multi-capture averaging — but for most surface/volume clinical endpoints, robust VIO+LiDAR fusion at proper distance is competitive markerless.

segmentation.current_mask_method: scripts/make_subject_masks.py produces a GEOMETRIC silhouette envelope, not a segmentation. Pipeline: (1) c* = least-squares intersection of camera optical axes (orbit convergence, background-independent, works at standoff); (2) keep SfM/metric init points within an ABSOLUTE 0.40 m radius of c* (metric-lock makes a physical radius valid); (3) per frame, project those cluster points, take their cv2.convexHull, fillConvexPoly, dilate ~4%; degenerate views fail OPEN (whole frame = subject). box.json's 3D box also drives a stage-2 Gaussian box-prune. Outputs 255=subject/0=background PNGs at color res. Structural limitations: (a) CONVEXITY — a convex hull cannot represent real anatomy, which is concave/non-convex (arch of a foot, gap between two feet or between fingers, region under a raised limb). The hull fills those concavities, so background between/around the anatomy is labeled 'subject': the photometric loss keeps supervising it and the flatness prior is wrongly disabled there. (b) OVER-INCLUSIVE BY DESIGN — generous pad, +4% dilation, fail-open, owner prefers under-cropping; anything immediately adjacent (the support stand, the table directly beneath, a clinician's hand) sits inside the envelope. (c) SfM-POINT DEPENDENT — the cluster is defined by triangulated points, but smooth skin yields few points while textured background (table grain, patterned drape) yields many, so the hull is pulled toward whatever got triangulated; low-texture extremities get under-covered. (d) SINGLE RIGID 3D BOX for all frames cannot adapt to shape or exclude an occluding hand/fixture within the radius. (e) NO SEMANTICS — it cannot distinguish the foot from the stand under it; both are geometry near c*. (f) ERROR COUPLING — pose error, center error, and especially the pipeline's known ~12% LiDAR/VIO scale ambiguity all move the box faces (a 12% scale error moves each face 12%). Net: it is a robust, pose/metric-derived subject LOCATOR, but a coarse over-inclusive convex ENVELOPE, not a true object boundary.
segmentation.would_seg_help: Yes, meaningfully, in all three areas — and it is complementary to (not a replacement for) the geometric locator. (1) SUBJECT-ISOLATION PRECISION: a true silhouette follows the real, concave object boundary, so the photometric loss stops supervising the concavity/adjacent background the convex hull currently includes. This directly attacks the pipeline's own 'doubled mesh' problem: remesh.py already fights fg/bg depth blending AT silhouette edges, and tighter per-frame masks mean fewer edge/near-silhouette floaters ever grow. Literature confirms accurate foreground masks improve geometry alignment and reduce floaters/incorrect-depth placement (Object-Centric 2DGS background loss; DeGauss; SpotLessSplats). (2) FLOATER/BACKGROUND REMOVAL: precise masks enable a mask-CONSISTENCY opacity prune — a Gaussian projecting outside the mask union across most views is background and can be pruned — which is strictly stronger than a convex box-prune. Clean-GS shows semantic masks from as few as ~1-3% of views remove background/floaters and compress models 60-80% while preserving the object. This layers on top of the existing box-prune and remesh de-doubling. (3) ANATOMY-vs-NOT-ANATOMY FOR THE FLATNESS PRIOR — the biggest, most specific win. The edge-aware prior (exp(-beta|grad I|)) already spares TEXTURED anatomy, so the mask's real job is protecting SMOOTH, low-gradient skin, which looks identical to a flat table/stand to the edge term. The convex hull GLUES the flat support to the foot, so the prior cannot flatten the stand where it lies inside the envelope — exactly backwards. An instance mask of the anatomy alone excludes the stand, so the flatten-allowed region becomes (inside 3D box) AND (outside anatomy mask) = the flat support the prior SHOULD flatten, while smooth skin stays hard-protected along its TRUE boundary. Caveat: SAM/SAM2 are class-agnostic — they segment 'a subject', not 'tissue'; they cannot by themselves label foot-vs-stand unless the prompt targets the anatomy. And zero-shot SAM2 on MEDICAL images is only 'modest / not clinically ready' — but that literature concerns low-contrast INTERNAL structures; here the task is external silhouette separation of a well-lit high-contrast limb from a stand, the easy regime, so those caveats mostly do not bind.
segmentation.seg_options: SAM2 (Meta, Apache-2.0): image+video with a streaming-memory transformer -> temporally CONSISTENT masks by design; prompt once (click or box) on a keyframe and propagate through the whole orbit; tiny->large checkpoints (~38-40+ FPS on GPU for the small variants, ~150MB-900MB). Best fit for an orbit-video capture and the 'one prompt -> consistent masks across all frames' need; server-side. Related 3DGS work uses SAM2 masks for consistent object grouping/segmentation (LabelGS; Gaussian-Grouping; SAM-mask-guided mesh+3DGS). MobileSAM: ~9.7M params (~66x smaller than SAM), ~10 FPS, mIoU ~0.74 (2-4 pts below SAM); IMAGE-only, no built-in temporal propagation -> must re-prompt per frame and will flicker across views; good only if you must run at capture on-device with no server. Apple Vision (on-device, free, nothing to ship): VNGeneratePersonSegmentationRequest (person-only, 3 quality levels, iOS 15+), VNGeneratePersonInstanceMask (iOS 17, <=4 people), and VNGenerateForegroundInstanceMaskRequest / VisionKit subject lifting (iOS 17+, CLASS-AGNOSTIC foreground). Person-segmentation is a surprisingly decent free anatomy-vs-support signal (a foot/hand/limb reads as 'person' and a non-body stand is excluded) but is single-image (no temporal memory -> per-frame flicker), version-dependent, a black box, and fails for non-body anatomy/specimens. DeepLabV3: older fixed-class (Pascal-VOC 21) semantic seg, CoreML-deployable, coarser boundaries, no instance/video; superseded by SAM2 (quality) and Apple Vision (free, on-device) for this use — not recommended. Prompting: single-POINT prompts are unreliable; BOX/click prompts are far more reliable in crowded/repetitive anatomy (SAM2-medical studies) — which favors deriving an automatic box prompt from the existing geometry rather than manual clicks.
segmentation.where_to_run: Recommend SERVER-SIDE at reconstruction time (SAM2 video on the A4000), with an OPTIONAL cheap on-device pass only for live capture UX. On-device at capture (Apple Vision or MobileSAM): pros — no frame upload, instant, can drive capture UX (show the user the live segmented subject, guide framing) and can be stored as a weak prior/backup prompt; cons — per-frame flicker (no temporal memory), weaker boundaries, no prompt propagation, another load inside the already-busy ARKit capture loop, and iOS-version/black-box dependence. Server-side at reconstruction (SAM2 video predictor): pros — temporal consistency via memory, best boundaries, can be prompted automatically from the geometric box, batched on the GPU, deterministic and versionable (matters for a clinical/reproducible pipeline), no iOS constraints; cons — adds one GPU stage and a model+checkpoint dependency. SAM2 inference is light versus training and fits the A4000 VRAM budget easily. For a clinical pipeline where mask quality and reproducibility outweigh latency, server-side SAM2 is the right home; the on-device Vision pass is a nice-to-have for UX and as a fallback prompt, not the authoritative mask.
segmentation.cost: Compute/latency: SAM2 video amortizes because you PROMPT ON KEYFRAMES and PROPAGATE — not per-frame from scratch (that is the whole advantage over per-image SAM/MobileSAM). SAM2-large is a few hundred ms/frame on an A4000, tiny/small much faster; a ~150-frame orbit is on the order of one to two minutes, once, server-side, well within VRAM. Integration effort: MODERATE. Add ONE new stage between make_subject_masks.py (which becomes the automatic PROMPTER) and stage-2, reusing the existing subject_masks/{fid}.png interface so stage-2 training, the box-prune, and remesh.py need no changes. New dependency: sam2 + a checkpoint (~150MB-900MB). Per-frame vs keyframe: keyframe-prompt + propagate (do NOT run per-frame segmentation independently). On-device option cost: Apple Vision is free/native (no model to ship, ~real-time) but single-image; MobileSAM adds a ~10-40MB model and ~10 FPS to the capture app. Preserve the current fail-open behavior as the fallback so the new stage can never make the pipeline worse.
segmentation.recommendation: Yes — add segmentation, but as a REFINEMENT of make_subject_masks.py, not a replacement, wired so it is fully automatic (no human click) and can only improve on the current masks. Concrete plan: (1) KEEP the geometric locator (c*, 0.40 m metric cluster, 3D box) — it is a robust pose/metric-derived subject finder that works at standoff where camera-hull culling fails. (2) Add a new server-side stage, e.g. scripts/refine_subject_masks_sam2.py, that uses box.json's projected 3D box on 1-3 keyframes as SAM2's BOX prompt (or the projected cluster centroid as a positive-point prompt), then runs the SAM2 VIDEO predictor to propagate temporally-consistent silhouettes across the whole orbit at color resolution. Intersect/gate the SAM2 mask with the projected 3D box so SAM2 cannot grab far background; write the same subject_masks/{fid}.png so downstream is untouched. Preserve fail-open: if SAM2 confidence is low or the mask is empty/implausibly small, fall back to the existing convex hull. (3) Feed the refined mask to BOTH consumers: (a) photometric-loss support (unchanged interface) and (b) a NEW mask-consistency opacity prune (Clean-GS style) to kill floaters, on top of the existing box-prune. (4) FLATNESS PRIOR (highest-value change): prompt SAM2 on the ANATOMY specifically so the mask excludes the support stand; define flatten-allowed = (inside 3D box) AND (outside anatomy mask). This flattens the flat stand/table the prior should flatten while hard-protecting smooth skin along its TRUE boundary instead of a convex envelope — fixing the current glue-stand-to-foot failure. (5) OPTIONAL: an on-device Apple Vision person/subject-lifting pass at capture for live UX and as a weak backup prompt — not the authoritative mask. Do NOT rely on SAM2 for tissue-level semantics or treat it as clinically validated; use it only for high-contrast external silhouette separation, which is its easy regime, and keep the geometry as the safety net and prompt source. Validate on a held-out capture by A/B-ing hull vs SAM2-refined masks on mesh doubling, floater count, and (with the ruler fiducial) metric accuracy before making it default.

synthesis.answer_flatness_masking:
HOW ANATOMY IS PROTECTED TODAY (geometric, no segmentation). Two mechanisms compose. (1) A geometric subject mask from scripts/make_subject_masks.py: c* = least-squares intersection of the camera optical axes (background-independent, works at standoff); keep metric-init points within an ABSOLUTE 0.40 m radius of c*; per frame project those points, then cv2.convexHull, fillConvexPoly, ~4% dilate; degenerate views fail OPEN (whole frame = subject). It also emits subject_masks/box.json (3D box) that drives the stage-2 Gaussian box-prune. This mask restricts the MILo photometric loss and the depth-regularization region to the subject. (2) The depth/flatness supervision itself is edge-aware: the AGS-Mesh edge-aware log-L1 LiDAR term (scripts/pose_ba/milo.patch, _ags.edge_aware_logl1, weight ~exp(-beta|grad I|)) down-weights depth flattening where image gradients are strong, so TEXTURED anatomy is spared automatically.

THE GAP. The edge term only protects textured surfaces. SMOOTH, low-gradient skin is indistinguishable from a flat table/stand to the edge term, so the ONLY thing protecting smooth skin is the mask, and today's mask is a convex hull. A hull cannot represent concave anatomy (foot arch, gaps between fingers/feet, under a raised limb) and is over-inclusive by design (generous pad, +4% dilation, fail-open), so it GLUES the flat support stand/table directly under the anatomy INTO the subject region. Net: it cannot tell the foot from the stand it rests on; the flattening the prior should apply to the flat stand is disabled exactly where the stand sits inside the envelope (backwards), and background trapped in concavities is still supervised.

WOULD SEGMENTATION IMPROVE IT: YES, materially, and this is the single highest-value change of the four. A true anatomy instance mask (SAM2, auto-prompted from the existing 3D box) follows the real concave boundary, so flatten-allowed becomes (inside 3D box) AND (outside anatomy mask) = flatten the flat stand/table the prior SHOULD flatten, while smooth skin is hard-protected along its TRUE boundary. The same mask tightens the photometric loss (stops supervising concavity background) and enables a mask-consistency opacity prune (Clean-GS style) strictly stronger than the box-prune. Keep the geometry as prompt-source + fail-open safety net. Caveat: SAM2 is class-agnostic, so prompt the anatomy specifically, and use it only for high-contrast external silhouette separation (its easy regime), never for tissue-level semantics.
synthesis.answer_4k_plus_lidar:
DEFINITIVE: YES, you can capture high-resolution color AND LiDAR depth concurrently. The teammate's "4K OR LiDAR, pick one" premise is not supported by any Apple documentation; color and depth are independent capture paths (4K RGB from the wide camera, depth from the LiDAR sensor) and supportsFrameSemantics(.sceneDepth) is a DEVICE-level check, not per-video-format. Two truths:

(1) APPLE-BLESSED, DEFINITELY WORKS (the path to ship). Run a normal LiDAR ARWorldTrackingConfiguration with .smoothedSceneDepth/.sceneDepth, set videoFormat = recommendedVideoFormatForHighResolutionFrameCapturing (a 1920x1440@60 stream), and pull 12MP (4032x3024) stills on demand via ARSession.captureHighResolutionFrame(completion:). Apple states the still is grabbed in the background while the video stream runs uninterrupted; depth stays available on that frame via frame.sceneDepth.depthMap. So you get continuous LiDAR depth + periodic 12MP color at NO either/or cost, and the stream stays at 60fps.

(2) CONTINUOUS 4K STREAM (recommendedVideoFormatFor4KResolution, 3840x2160@30) + per-frame sceneDepth: architecturally fine but UNDOCUMENTED by Apple and unproven by any first-party sample. Treat as unverified; settle it on-device with a one-liner: set config.videoFormat = recommendedVideoFormatFor4KResolution, add .sceneDepth, run, confirm session.currentFrame?.sceneDepth != nil across several frames.

KEY FACTS. Only ONE videoFormat is active at a time, so the 4K-stream format and the high-res-capture format are mutually exclusive; THIS format-level either/or (not a color-vs-LiDAR one) is the likely origin of the teammate's belief. Depth does NOT scale with color: sceneDepth.depthMap is always 256x192. Tradeoffs: the continuous 4K format halves everything to 30fps (depth+pose cadence too); captureHighResolutionFrame keeps 60fps and costs only a brief still. Requires iPhone Pro (12 Pro or newer) on iOS 16+. Gotchas: do NOT pass custom AVCapturePhotoSettings.isDepthDataDeliveryEnabled to captureHighResolutionFrame (unsupported, throws) and do NOT read frame.capturedDepthData (TrueDepth/face-only); use default settings + frame.sceneDepth.depthMap. Verify the 256x192 sceneDepth registers correctly to the 12MP capturedImage (alignment bug reported on some iPad Pro units) before trusting metric depth on stills.

RECOMMENDATION FOR THIS APP: implement "ARKit-4K" as the high-res-frame-capture path (1440p@60 + smoothedSceneDepth stream, 12MP still pulled on the keyframe gate), NOT the continuous 4K format; it is Apple-confirmed, keeps 60fps depth, and preserves per-frame LiDAR.
synthesis.answer_markerless_metric:
DEFINITIVE: YES for mm-scale SHAPE and few-percent absolute metric WITHOUT any physical marker; NO for a hard 1mm / sub-1% absolute guarantee from phone sensors alone. Mechanism: monocular SfM/GS is fixed only up to a 7-DoF similarity (3 translation, 3 rotation, 1 global scale); reprojection is blind ONLY to the single global-scale scalar, exactly your own finding (three pose solutions agree to ~1-2mm, 1.8px reproj pins relative geometry sub-mm). So shape is already sub-mm; the one thing images cannot fix is metric scale, which needs an EXTERNAL metric reference. You already carry two markerless ones, no ruler required:

PRIMARY = ARKit VIO camera-path baseline. Gravity-anchored, metric, immune to LiDAR near-field bias (this is why your camera_path=1.000 over LiDAR=1.127 choice was correct). Realistic scale error ~2-5% WITH motion excitation; but scale is only observable when translational acceleration is non-zero, so a static hover / tiny straight slide is poorly conditioned (straight-line 9.2% down to figure-eight 4.8%). Prescribe a figure-eight / varied-curvature orbit.

SECONDARY = LiDAR, used ONLY at 250mm-or-greater working distance (rear LiDAR reliable min range ~0.25m), via a robust MEDIAN of the LiDAR/SfM depth ratio over high-confidence pixels + temporal averaging across frames. Your ~12% LiDAR-vs-VIO gap at 203mm is a real near-field CAPTURE-DISTANCE artifact (capture sat below LiDAR min range), shrinking to ~3.5% at 323mm; not a pipeline defect. Below 250mm, defer to VIO.

FLOORS: LiDAR ~+/-1cm absolute for objects over 10cm (fails under ~5cm); VIO scale ~2-5%; ruler / known-size fiducial under 1% / sub-mm. Best-case markerless fusion (250mm-or-greater LiDAR median anchor + well-excited VIO + temporal averaging) reaches the ~2-4% band = a few mm on a 10cm feature, right at the edge of the clinical +/-2mm bar, NOT a guaranteed 1mm. Vectra's edge is a permanently calibrated stereo baseline = a hardwired metric "marker"; your differentiator is portable + markerless + full-surface GS at a realistic cost of few-percent absolute scale.

RULER = VALIDATION-ONLY: keep any ruler / known-size fiducial OUT of the clinical scene; run it periodically as ground-truth QA to measure residual absolute-scale error and detect drift, never as a required in-frame marker. If a specific endpoint truly needs 1mm absolute, the only honest markerless-adjacent options are a one-time known-size calibration per session or multi-capture averaging. Market/validate against the ~1-3mm smartphone-vs-Vectra clinical band, not a 1mm claim.
synthesis.answer_segmentation:
RECOMMENDATION: YES, add segmentation, but as a REFINEMENT of scripts/make_subject_masks.py (not a replacement), fully automatic (no human click), wired so it can only improve on today's masks. Use SAM2 (Meta, Apache-2.0) SERVER-SIDE on the A4000 at reconstruction time: its video predictor gives temporally consistent masks by prompt-once-propagate (a ~150-frame orbit is ~1-2 min, well within VRAM), it is deterministic/versionable (matters clinically), and it avoids adding load to the ARKit capture loop. Concrete plan:

1. KEEP the geometric locator (c*, 0.40 m metric cluster, 3D box); a robust pose/metric-derived subject finder that works at standoff where camera-hull culling fails. It becomes the automatic PROMPTER.
2. NEW stage scripts/refine_subject_masks_sam2.py: use box.json's projected 3D box on 1-3 keyframes as SAM2's BOX prompt (box/click prompts are far more reliable than single points in repetitive anatomy), propagate across the orbit, intersect/gate the SAM2 mask with the projected 3D box so it cannot grab far background, and write the SAME subject_masks/{fid}.png, so stage-2 training, the box-prune, and remesh.py need ZERO changes.
3. PRESERVE fail-open: low SAM2 confidence or an empty/implausibly-small mask falls back to the existing convex hull. The new stage can never make the pipeline worse.
4. Feed the refined mask to BOTH consumers: (a) photometric-loss support (unchanged interface) and (b) a NEW mask-consistency opacity prune (Clean-GS style) to kill floaters, on top of the box-prune.
5. HIGHEST-VALUE use: prompt the ANATOMY specifically so the mask excludes the support stand; define flatten-allowed = (inside 3D box) AND (outside anatomy mask). This fixes the glue-stand-to-foot failure and hard-protects smooth skin along its TRUE boundary.
6. OPTIONAL: an on-device Apple Vision subject-lifting / person-segmentation pass at capture for live framing UX and as a weak backup prompt, NOT the authoritative mask.

Do NOT treat SAM2 as clinically validated or use it for tissue-level semantics; use it only for high-contrast external silhouette separation, which is its easy regime. On-device MobileSAM (per-frame flicker, no temporal memory) and DeepLabV3 (fixed-class, superseded) are not recommended for the authoritative mask. VALIDATE by A/B-ing hull vs SAM2-refined masks on mesh doubling, floater count, and (with the ruler-QA fiducial) metric accuracy before making it default.

synthesis.capture_day_plan:
RENTED-VECTRA CAPTURE DAY. Goal: (a) get sub-mm ground-truth to validate the markerless few-percent metric claim, (b) A/B the segmentation/flatness win against truth, (c) settle the 4K+LiDAR question on-device.

PHANTOM / OBJECTS. Bring a RIGID phantom with certified/known geometry (a machined or 3D-printed step-block, a calibration sphere, or a printed object with a stated dimension); this gives absolute ground-truth WITHOUT putting a marker in the clinical frame. Plus one anatomy-like target (a foot/hand model or a volunteer limb) resting ON a support stand, to reproduce the glue-stand-to-foot case the segmentation change targets. Label every capture with the in-app Description field so each phone session pairs cleanly with its Vectra scan.

RULER = VALIDATION-ONLY. Shoot the ruler / known-size fiducial in a SEPARATE calibration clip (or only at the frame edge), purely to quantify residual absolute-scale error. Never in the clinical anatomy scene; it is QA, not a required in-frame marker.

MODES TO CAPTURE (same subject, back-to-back; default working distance 250mm or greater so LiDAR is in-range; use a FIGURE-EIGHT / varied-curvature orbit for VIO scale excitation, not a static hover or straight slide):
  1. ARKit-1080 + LiDAR (today's default; baseline).
  2. ARKit-4K + LiDAR (high-res-frame-capture path); during this run do the on-device checks: session.currentFrame?.sceneDepth != nil across frames, and eyeball 256x192 sceneDepth-to-12MP alignment.
  3. HQ-stills + LiDAR (if implemented; else HQ-Depth as-is).
  4. One ARKit + LiDAR-OFF (RGB-only) pass; exercises the new LiDAR-off frame path and pose-free SfM scale.
  5. A dedicated ruler / known-size calibration clip (validation-only).
  6. One deliberate under-250mm pass on the phantom; to reproduce and quantify the near-field LiDAR bias against Vectra truth.

VECTRA SCAN. Scan the SAME phantom + anatomy target on the rented Vectra as metric ground-truth (permanently calibrated stereo baseline, sub-mm). Record the landmark set / surface region so the phone reconstructions can be registered to it.

ANALYSIS (after the day). For each mode run the pipeline, then produce a per-mode table: (1) absolute scale error vs Vectra AND vs the known phantom dimension, which quantifies VIO-primary vs LiDAR-anchor scale and confirms the 250mm-or-greater rule and the under-250mm degradation; (2) surface RMS vs Vectra; (3) convex-hull masks vs SAM2-refined masks A/B on mesh-doubling, floater count, and metric error, with Vectra as truth (validates the segmentation stage before making it default); (4) does 4K color improve reconstruction fidelity enough to justify the still-capture cost / thermal load; (5) log thermal throttling over a sustained clinical-length capture. Deliverable: that table sets the default capture mode and either confirms or bounds the markerless few-percent absolute-scale claim.

verification.arkit_claim_solid: WELL-SUPPORTED on substance; overstated only in wording. The path the plan actually ships — ARWorldTrackingConfiguration + .smoothedSceneDepth/.sceneDepth, videoFormat = recommendedVideoFormatForHighResolutionFrameCapturing (1920x1440@60 stream), 12MP stills via ARSession.captureHighResolutionFrame(completion:), depth read from frame.sceneDepth.depthMap — is real and Apple-documented. I web-verified forum thread 805839 (capturedDepthData is nil on the high-res frame by design; you use sceneDepth.depthMap, exactly as the synthesis says) and thread 808028: the sceneDepth-to-capturedImage registration bug is real on the 2022 iPad Pro 6th-gen BUT "on iPhone 16 Pro depth is correctly registered for both standard and high-resolution captures." Since the target is iPhone Pro, the load-bearing alignment caveat actually resolves in the app's favor. Device/OS (iPhone 12 Pro+, iOS 16+), depth staying 256x192 regardless of RGB, and the teammate's "4K-OR-LiDAR" being a format-level (one videoFormat active) not a color-vs-LiDAR either/or — all correct. Over-reaches: (1) the rhetoric "DEFINITIVE / Apple-blessed, DEFINITELY WORKS" is stronger than a single forum thread + a device-specific registration bug warrants (fine in practice on iPhone, but it is forum-sourced, not formal doc, that depth is registered on the 12MP frame). (2) Labeling this "ARKit-4K" is misleading: it is a 1440p@60 stream plus a 12MP (4032x3024) on-demand still — higher than 4K on the still, but NOT a continuous 4K stream. The synthesis correctly flags the continuous recommendedVideoFormatFor4KResolution (3840x2160@30) + per-frame sceneDepth path as undocumented/unverified and tells you to settle it on-device. No API is misused. No missed hard device/OS caveat.
verification.metric_claim_solid: DIRECTIONALLY DEFENSIBLE, but optimistic in four specific spots; the headline conclusion (no guaranteed 1mm-absolute markerless from phone sensors; realistic few-percent scale) is honest and correct. Over-reaches: (1) "shape is already sub-mm" conflates PRECISION with ACCURACY. The internal finding (three pose solutions agree ~1-2mm, 1.8px reproj) shows the SfM/GS geometry is self-consistent/repeatable to ~1-2mm, not that the reconstructed SURFACE is sub-mm accurate vs a metric reference; GS/photometric surfaces carry their own bias on smooth/textureless skin and floaters. Defensible statement: ~1-2mm relative precision, absolute surface accuracy unvalidated. (2) The few-percent-to-mm arithmetic is optimistic: 3% scale error on a 100mm feature is 3mm, which ALREADY EXCEEDS the +/-2mm bar (not "right at the edge"), and scale error compounds — 2-4% on a 200mm span is 4-8mm. +/-2mm is met only for sub-~7cm features. (3) The 9.2%/6.4%/4.8% straight/circular/figure-eight numbers are from a REAL, web-verified paper (arXiv 2603.26740, "Motion as a Sensing Modality...", 2026-03-22), but were measured on a DIFFERENTIAL-DRIVE GROUND ROBOT with meter-scale trajectories and a consumer IMU — not a clinician's handheld close-range (250mm) orbit of a 10cm target. The direction (excite with curvature/figure-eight) transfers; the exact 4.8% floor does not necessarily. (4) LiDAR "~1-2% at >=250mm" is best-case: the documented absolute floor is ~1cm for >10cm objects (Luetzenburg/PMC8593014), which is ~4% at a 250mm standoff; robust median + temporal averaging is the route to few-percent but 1-2% is optimistic as a typical figure. Defensible floor to market/validate against: ~1-2mm relative shape precision; absolute scale realistically 3-5% typical, ~2% best-case fused (well-excited VIO + >=250mm LiDAR median + temporal averaging); +/-2mm absolute only on small features; 1mm absolute NOT markerless-achievable — matching the 1-3mm smartphone-vs-Vectra clinical band with Bland-Altman LoA often >3mm that the raw itself cites.
app_plan_risks: FIRST-CUT arkit4K == arkit1080 (validity trap): on a LiDAR iPhone Pro the DEFAULT ARWorldTrackingConfiguration.videoFormat is already ~1920x1440@60, and recommendedVideoFormatForHighResolutionFrameCapturing is ALSO 1920x1440@60. The plan's own 'minimal first cut' ships arkit4K as just the 1440p stream and DEFERS the 12MP captureHighResolutionFrame still. Until that deferred, non-trivial async still lands, arkit4K and arkit1080 (which 'leaves default') produce IDENTICAL data. The capture-day A/B of '1080 vs 4K' (capture_day_plan modes 1 vs 2) would compare two identical configs. The only real resolution difference IS the deferred piece.

HQ-stills coexistence under-flagged: the plan adds an AVCapturePhotoOutput with isDepthDataDeliveryEnabled=true to the SAME AVCaptureSession (AVFoundationCaptureSource.swift, configureIfNeeded 79-154) that already streams AVCaptureDepthDataOutput on .builtInLiDARDepthCamera with sessionPreset=.inputPriority and a hand-locked activeFormat. Simultaneous streaming-depth + photo-depth on one session may fail canAddOutput()/conflict, and the still resolution is bounded by the manually-locked activeFormat (picked for max depth-capable VIDEO at lines 116-123, not sensor-max photo) so 'full-res still' may not exceed the existing HQ video res. This risk is flagged LESS than the arkit4K still, but is comparably non-trivial.

LiDAR-off ARKit path correctness: providesPose MUST stay TRUE for ARKit RGB-only (VIO pose from frame.camera.transform survives without LiDAR); if set false it wrongly triggers unseeded SfM on the Linux side. The synthesized all-NaN depth array must be sized depthW*depthH exactly or FrameWriter.writeFrame drops the frame (guard at FrameWriter.swift:58 'depth.count == depthW*depthH'). depth_source='none (RGB only)' must be applied at the actual construction site CaptureModel.stopRecording (262-264), which the plan describes but does not pin. mask=all-255 makes the UI validDepthFraction read 100% (misleading but cosmetic).

Frame-cap refactor concurrency constraint: maxKeyframes/budgetSeconds/targetFrames are read from SessionCoordinator.startWriting (delegate queue, nonisolated) and AVFoundationCaptureSource.startWriting (dataQueue, nonisolated) via the weak @MainActor CaptureModel. Under Swift 6 strict concurrency this compiles ONLY if they stay `let` (Sendable) constants — a `var` would be a cross-actor-access error. Plan says 'constants', so OK, but it is a hard requirement, and timeStride MUST be recomputed as budgetSeconds/targetFrames (KeyframeSelector currently hardcodes 20.0/48 at line 13).

The critical invariant the plan DID catch (no error, confirming): KeyframeSelector.budgetSeconds (used by isFinished, KeyframeSelector.swift:37/44) must equal CaptureModel.budgetSeconds (safetyStop backstop at CaptureModel.swift:204 = +1s), else capture stops at the smaller. Verified accurate.
corrections: All app-plan file paths, symbols, and line numbers were verified against current source and are CORRECT — no wrong files/symbols and no ARKit/AVFoundation API misuse. recommendedVideoFormatForHighResolutionFrameCapturing and captureHighResolutionFrame are real; the plan's explicit avoidance of recommendedVideoFormatFor4KResolution (30fps, undocumented-with-depth) is well-founded. The plan's risks are implementation/validity, not misuse.

Downgrade 'shape is already sub-mm' to '~1-2mm relative/self-consistent precision; absolute surface accuracy vs a metric reference unvalidated.' Reproducibility across pose solutions is not validated surface accuracy.

Fix the scale-to-mm framing: a few-percent scale error is NOT 'right at the edge of +/-2mm' generally — 3% of a 100mm feature = 3mm (over the bar), compounding to 4-8mm on 200mm spans. +/-2mm is met only for sub-~7cm features.

Re-caveat the figure-eight/4.8% numbers: the source (arXiv 2603.26740, verified real) measured them on a differential-drive ground robot at meter scale with a consumer IMU, not a handheld 250mm anatomy orbit. Keep the qualitative prescription (excite with curvature), drop the exact 4.8% as a transferable floor.
Soften LiDAR 'gets you ~1-2% at >=250mm': the documented floor is ~1cm for >10cm objects (~4% at 250mm); 1-2% is best-case via robust median + temporal averaging, not typical.

Soften the 4K+LiDAR 'DEFINITIVE / DEFINITELY WORKS' to 'supported on iPhone Pro; the 12MP-still-carries-registered-sceneDepth fact is forum-sourced (thread 805839) and had a registration bug on 2022 iPad Pro that resolves on iPhone 16 Pro — verify on your exact device.' And rename 'ARKit-4K' since it is 1440p stream + 12MP still, and the differentiating still is deferred.
verification.verdict: 4K+LiDAR concurrency: TRUE and well-supported for the path the plan actually ships (1440p@60 stream + 12MP captureHighResolutionFrame still + sceneDepth on iPhone Pro / iOS 16+); web-verified against Apple forums, and the one load-bearing alignment caveat resolves favorably on iPhone. Overstated only in rhetoric ('DEFINITIVE') and in calling it '4K' (it is 1440p + 12MP still, and that differentiating still is deferred to a fast-follow, making first-cut arkit4K identical to arkit1080). Markerless mm-metric: the headline (no guaranteed 1mm absolute; realistic few-percent scale; refuse the 1mm claim) is HONEST and defensible, but it over-reaches by (a) calling ~1-2mm relative PRECISION 'sub-mm shape', (b) implying few-percent scale sits 'at the edge' of +/-2mm when 3% already exceeds it on a 10cm feature and compounds on larger spans, (c) transplanting a ground-robot's 4.8% figure-eight number onto a handheld close-range orbit, and (d) quoting LiDAR 1-2% where ~1cm/~4% at 250mm is the documented floor. App edit plan: NO wrong files/symbols and NO API misuse — every path, symbol, and line number checks out; residual risks are implementation/validity (first-cut arkit4K==arkit1080, HQ-stills photo+stream-depth coexistence, LiDAR-off must keep providesPose=true and correctly-sized NaN depth, and keeping the cap constants as `let` for cross-queue reads).
